candidates.title: Research Radar skill
candidates.summary: Package repeatable research workflows as a Codex skill with structured source quality, risk, and relevance scoring.
evidence: Codex skills package instructions, resources, and optional scripts for reusable workflows.
Skills use progressive disclosure, so rich workflows can stay available without bloating context upfront.

candidates.title: Codex subagent audit profiles
candidates.summary: Define read-only research and audit subagents for official docs, compatibility risk, implementation fit, alternatives, and roadmap synthesis.
evidence: Codex can spawn specialized agents in parallel and collect results in one response.
Codex only spawns subagents when explicitly asked.
concerns: Subagent workflows consume more tokens than comparable single-agent runs.

candidates.title: Sleep Curator trigger pack
candidates.summary: Use Codex Automations and worktrees to schedule memory compaction and reflection tasks.
evidence: Codex Automations can use plugins and skills, and project automations can run in a dedicated worktree.
concerns: Background automation with broad write access carries elevated risk.

candidates.title: Strict hooks mode
candidates.summary: Make governance hooks blocking by default for iteration compliance and drift checks.
evidence: Codex hooks can run deterministic scripts during lifecycle events.
concerns: Hooks are experimental and may create workflow friction.

candidates.title: Local memories as source of truth
candidates.summary: Use local memories as the primary enforcement layer for team project rules.
evidence: Codex Memories are useful as a local recall layer.
concerns: Required team guidance should remain in AGENTS.md or checked-in documentation.
